title: System.Uri arguments should be preferred instead of strings
description: "System.Uri" arguments should be used instead of stringsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?String representations of URIs or URLs are prone to parsing and encoding errors which can lead to vulnerabilities. TheSystem.Uriclass is a safe alternative and should be preferred.This rule raises an issue when a called method has a string parameter with a name containing "uri", "Uri", "urn", "Urn", "url" or "Url" and the declaring type contains a corresponding overload that takes aSystem.Urias a parameter.When there is a choice between two overloads that differ only regarding the representation of a URI, the user should choose the overload that takes aSystem.Uriargument.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo {
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use System.Uri instead of string for URI arguments - prefer the Uri overload

logic.reasoning: Detects method calls with string URI arguments where a Uri overload would be safer. Excludes Uri constructors and logging.
